subject: english
text:
"What song shall I sing of you, my mother?"
A) Who does I refer to ?
B) Who is the mother here ?
C) What type of song does the poet wish to sing?

subject: english
text:
“Sing of the beggar and leper.”
A) Who is the speaker ?
B) Who has to sing about these people ?
C) Where do we see these people ?

subject: english
text:
"Shall I sing of your rock cut temple, ‘epics in stone’.
A) Why does the poet call the temples "epics in stone"?
B) What is the figure of speech used here ?
C) What do you mean by ‘an epic’?

subject: english
text:
Of your children that died to call you their own, their very own.
A) Who is the 'you', here ?
B) Who are the children here ?
C) Why did the children die ?

subject: english
text:
That beat into my ears like gong
A) Who is the speaker?
B) What does the word 'that' refer to?
C) What is the figure of speech used here?

subject: english
text:
"Is there no song that I can sing of you
Heart -whole unalloyed?"
A) Who does ‘I’ refer to ?
B) What feeling does the speaker express here?
C) What type of song does the ‘I’ want to sing?

subject: english
text:
"Nervous, I yet would ask deeming it my task"
A) Who made this statement?
B) Why was the speaker nervous ?
C) What did he deem as his  task ?

subject: english
text:
"She sat and wrote the book of Morrow"
A) Who is the speaker?
B) Who wrote the book of Morrow?
C) What does the book of Morrow symbolise?

text:

text: Narrate the conversation between the poet V. K. Gokak and mother India.
